mappings.reason: Require MFA for Remote Network Access directly implements the strong authentication requirement for remote maintenance sessions.
Require MFA for Administrative Access directly implements the strong authentication requirement for maintenance activities which are administrative in nature.
Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications addresses strong authentication for remote tools if they are exposed externally, covering a subset of remote maintenance scenarios.
Require users to authenticate to enterprise-managed VPN prior to accessing enterprise resources enforces authentication for remote device connections used in maintenance.
Collect Command-Line Audit Logs records the commands executed during remote maintenance sessions, satisfying the session recording requirement for CLI-based maintenance.
Configure Detailed Audit Logs captures session metadata (source, destination, user, timestamp) necessary to record remote maintenance activity.
Collect Audit Logs establishes the foundational logging capability required to record remote sessions, providing coverage under Rule 6.
Securely Manage Enterprise Assets restricts remote management to secure protocols (SSH/HTTPS) and prohibits insecure ones, aligning with the restriction of tool utilization.
Securely Manage Network Infrastructure restricts remote management of network devices to secure protocols, covering the network infrastructure subset of remote maintenance.
Establish Dedicated Computing Resources for Administrative Work restricts where maintenance activities occur, supporting the restriction to minimum required level.
